  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What level of cleanup do you want? A) Line-level copyedit only (grammar/concision/terminology consistency; no claim changes), B) Copyedit plus resolve logical inconsistencies and temper overstatements (may lightly restructure paragraphs), or C) Deeper conceptual reframing of the contribution and section-level reorganization.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "There is an apparent tension between statements about deployment failure and being well-handled via online recalibration that likely requires logical consistency cleanup.",
    "R1.6": "The phrase 'Clean this up' most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level editing for clarity, concision, and flow.",
    "RX.1": "The request is underspecified, so we should surface uncertainty about scope and confirm the intended depth of edits.",
    "RX.2": "Cleanup should avoid introducing or preserving overclaims, especially around performance and handling of protocol mismatches.",
    "RX.3": "We must adhere to the user's requested scope and avoid introducing new content or results beyond cleanup.",
    "RX.4": "Edits to safety- and deployment-relevant claims should minimize risk of misleading guidance or misuse.",
    "RX.5": "Any edits should maintain traceability to the original text so changes can be reviewed or reverted."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What level of cleanup do you want? A) Line-level copyedit only (grammar/concision/terminology consistency; no claim changes), B) Copyedit plus resolve logical inconsistencies and temper overstatements (may lightly restructure paragraphs), or C) Deeper conceptual reframing of the contribution and section-level reorganization.

rationale.R1.2: There is an apparent tension between statements about deployment failure and being well-handled via online recalibration that likely requires logical consistency cleanup.
rationale.R1.6: The phrase 'Clean this up' most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level editing for clarity, concision, and flow.
rationale.RX.1: The request is underspecified, so we should surface uncertainty about scope and confirm the intended depth of edits.
rationale.RX.2: Cleanup should avoid introducing or preserving overclaims, especially around performance and handling of protocol mismatches.
rationale.RX.3: We must adhere to the user's requested scope and avoid introducing new content or results beyond cleanup.
rationale.RX.4: Edits to safety- and deployment-relevant claims should minimize risk of misleading guidance or misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Any edits should maintain traceability to the original text so changes can be reviewed or reverted.
